factors.summary: Overall demand appears stable with mixed evidence from different segments.

evidence.text: Revenue growth in the Mining, Industrial, Infrastructure & Power, Government and Diversified Services segments was partially offset by revenue declines in the Energy & Chemicals segment. Consolidated revenue of $9.7 billion for the six months ended June 30, 2018 increased slightly compared to $9.6 billion for the six months ended June 30, 2017.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand is stable with mixed evidence from different segments.

factors.summary: Order backlog decreased due to project cancellations and work performed outpacing new awards.

evidence.text: Consolidated backlog as of June 30, 2018 was $29.3 billion compared to $37.6 billion as of June 30, 2017. The decrease in backlog primarily resulted from the removal of a nuclear power plant project for Westinghouse Electric Company LLC (Westinghouse) as well as new award activity being outpaced by work performed.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Order backlog decreased due to project cancellations and work outpacing new awards.

factors.summary: The company faces downward pressure on pricing due to intense competition.

evidence.text: Intense competition in the global engineering, procurement and construction industry, which can place downward pressure on our contract prices and profit margins and may increase our contractual risks;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Intense competition is placing downward pressure on pricing and margins.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, negatively impacting overall margins.

evidence.text: Revenue for the three and six months ended June 30, 2018 decreased by 6 percent and 8 percent, respectively, compared to the corresponding periods in 2017, primarily due to reduced volume of project execution activity for several chemicals and downstream projects that were nearing completion in 2017. This revenue decline was partially offset by an increase in project execution activities for a large upstream project.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, negatively impacting margins.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to challenges in certain segments.

evidence.text: Consolidated revenue of $4.9 billion for the three months ended June 30, 2018 increased 4 percent compared to $4.7 billion for the three months ended June 30, 2017. Revenue growth in the Mining, Industrial, Infrastructure & Power, Government and Diversified Services segments was partially offset by revenue declines in the Energy & Chemicals segment.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, indicating moderate headwinds.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed across segments, with some areas showing growth and others decline.

evidence.text: Revenue for the three months ended June 30, 2018 increased by 13 percent compared to the same period in 2017. Revenue growth resulted primarily from new mining projects and increased project execution activity for certain existing mining & metals, infrastructure and life sciences & advance manufacturing projects.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Volume trends show growth in certain segments, indicating positive momentum.

factors.summary: Government and certain infrastructure segments are growing faster than others.

evidence.text: Revenue for the three and six months ended June 30, 2018 increased 16 percent and 45 percent, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2017. The revenue growth in both periods resulted primarily from project execution activities for a power restoration project in Puerto Rico, which commenced in the fourth quarter of 2017 and was substantially completed in the first half of 2018, as well as increased hurricane relief efforts for the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Government and infrastructure segments are growing faster than others, showing positive market momentum.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable as there's no mention of significant changes in raw materials or commodities.

evidence.text: The company holds cash in bank deposits and marketable securities which are governed by the company's investment policy. This policy focuses on, in order of priority, the preservation of capital, maintenance of liquidity and maximization of yield.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are stable with no significant changes mentioned.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with no significant mention of wage inflation or benefit expense issues.

evidence.text: The company's effective tax rates for the three and six months ended June 30, 2018 were 28.6 percent and 31.8 percent, respectively, compared to 72.3 percent and (1.8) percent for the corresponding periods of 2017.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable without significant issues.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to cost overruns and project execution challenges.

evidence.text: Segment profit margin for the three and six months ended June 30, 2018 was 4.8 percent and 5.1 percent compared to 5.7 percent and 4.9 percent for the same periods in the prior year. The changes in segment profit margin in both periods were primarily due to the same factors that drove the changes in segment profit.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to cost overruns and project execution challenges.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are in place but their impact is limited by project execution challenges.

evidence.text: The company adopted Accounting Standards Codification (ASC) Topic 606 Revenue from Contracts with Customers on January 1, 2018. The impact of adoption was an increase to the company's revenue for the three and six months ended June 30, 2018 of $30 million and $32 million, respectively, primarily in the Energy & Chemicals segment.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs are in place but their impact is limited by project execution challenges.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving through project execution and cost management.

evidence.text: Excluding the non-cash impact of adopting ASC 606, working capital increased primarily due to an increase in contract assets and a decrease in contract liabilities partially offset by an increase in accounts payable during the six months ended June 30, 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency is improving through project execution and cost management.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are maintained at a moderate level, indicating cautious growth investment.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures of $110 million and $142 million for the six months ended June 30, 2018 and 2017, respectively, primarily related to construction equipment associated with equipment operations in the Diversified Services segment, as well as expenditures for facilities and investments in information technology.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are maintained at a moderate level, indicating cautious growth investment.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging with strong credit ratings and access to capital markets.

evidence.text: The company has both committed and uncommitted lines of credit available to be used for revolving loans and letters of credit. The company believes that for at least the next 12 months, cash generated from operations, along with its unused credit capacity and substantial cash position, is sufficient to support operating requirements. As of June 30, 2018, the company was in compliance with all the financial covenants related to its debt agreements.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is deleveraging with strong credit ratings and access to capital markets.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its dividend payments, indicating confidence in its financial position.

evidence.text: Quarterly cash dividends are typically paid during the month following the quarter in which they are declared. Therefore, dividends declared in the fourth quarter of 2017 were paid in the first quarter of 2018. Quarterly cash dividends of $0.21 per share were declared in the second quarter of 2018 and 2017. The payment and level of future cash dividends is subject to the discretion of the company's Board of Directors.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is maintaining its dividend payments, indicating confidence in its financial position.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable with cash utilized by operating activities.

evidence.text: Cash utilized by operating activities was $133 million for the six months ended June 30, 2018 compared to cash provided by operating activities of $428 million for the corresponding period of the prior year. The decrease in cash from operating activities resulted primarily from declines in net working capital inflows.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow generation is stable with cash utilized by operating activities.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is limited, with no major new acquisitions mentioned.

evidence.text: The company did not consider any cash to be permanently reinvested overseas as of June 30, 2018 and December 31, 2017 and, as a result, has appropriately reflected the tax impact on foreign earnings in deferred taxes.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Limited acquisition activity suggests no significant changes in capital allocation strategy.

factors.summary: Management has not provided specific forward guidance in the text.

evidence.text: The company cannot control such risk factors and other uncertainties, and in many cases, cannot predict the risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those indicated by the forward-looking statements.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No specific forward guidance provided by management.

factors.summary: Management confidence is cautious due to risks and uncertainties mentioned, such as project cancellations and economic conditions.

evidence.text: The company's actual results may differ materially from its expectations or projections. Due to known and unknown risks, the company's actual results may differ materially from its expectations or projections.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses caution due to risks and uncertainties affecting results.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include project cancellations, economic downturns, and cybersecurity threats.

evidence.text: · Project cancellations, scope adjustments or deferrals, or foreign currency fluctuations, that could reduce the amount of our backlog and the revenue and profits that the company earns;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include project cancellations, economic downturns, and cybersecurity threats, indicating potential challenges.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on diversification and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The company now reports its operating results in the following four reportable segments: Energy & Chemicals; Mining, Industrial, Infrastructure & Power; Government; and Diversified Services. Segment operating information and assets for 2017 have been recast to reflect these changes.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is focusing on diversification and operational efficiency, but the evidence doesn't show clear positive or negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign currency fluctuations, economic conditions, and project execution risks.

evidence.text: The effective tax rates for the three and six months ended June 30, 2018 were 28.6 percent and 31.8 percent, respectively, compared to 72.3 percent and (1.8) percent for the corresponding periods of 2017. The effective rates for the three and six months ended June 30, 2018, which reflected the lower U.S. corporate income tax rate enacted by the Tax Act, were unfavorably impacted by higher tax rates on foreign earnings and the inability to offset the losses recognized in certain jurisdictions against the income recognized in other jurisdictions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces significant sensitivities to foreign currency fluctuations, economic conditions, and project execution risks, which could negatively impact performance.

factors.summary: A potential catalyst is the DOE award for nuclear technology development.

evidence.text: In April 2018, the DOE announced that NuScale had been selected to receive an additional cost sharing award for $40 million under the DOE's Office of Nuclear Energy's U.S. Industry Opportunities for Advanced Nuclear Technology Development funding opportunity announcement (FOA).

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Receipt of a DOE award for nuclear technology development presents a potential growth opportunity.
